title: Hasbro confirmed a cyberattack and says recovery could take several weeks
author: Ellsworth Toohey
summary: Hasbro, the company behind Transformers, Dungeons & Dragons, Monopoly, Magic: The Gathering, and My Little Pony, confirmed a cyberattack after detecting unauthorized network access on March 28 and disclosed the incident to the SE
full_text:
Hasbro, the company behind Transformers, Dungeons & Dragons, Monopoly, Magic: The Gathering, and My Little Pony, confirmed a cyberattack after detecting unauthorized network access on March 28 and disclosed the incident to the SEC on April 1. The company took some systems offline, hired third-party cybersecurity experts, and says recovery could take "several weeks" while it assesses the full scope.

Hasbro has activated business continuity plans to keep taking orders, shipping products, and running key operations, though some delays are expected, and parts of its website remain down. Spokesperson Andrea Snyder said the company "took swift action to protect our systems and data," but declined to specify the type of attack or reveal any communications with hackers.

It's still unknown whether data was stolen, with the investigation ongoing to review potentially affected files. The "several weeks" timeline raises concerns for Hasbro's 5,000+ employees, as seen in Jaguar Land Rover's 2025 attack that halted production for months and required a $1.5 billion UK government backstop.
